description: Table misc du mod TCP (colonnes documentees via le D2R Data Guide).
source: Colonnes du .txt (mod TCP) ; types/descriptions du D2R Data Guide (guide/).

columns.name: name
columns.description: This is a reference field to define the item; it does not need to be uniquely named

columns.name: compactsave
columns.description: If equals 1, then only the item's base stats will be stored in the character save, but not any modifiers or additional stats. If equals 0, then all of the items stats will be saved.

columns.name: version
columns.description: Defines which game version to create this item (0 = Classic mode | 100 = Expansion mode)

columns.name: level
columns.description: Controls the base item level. This is used for determining when the item is allowed to drop, such as making sure that the item level is not greater than the monster's level or the area level.

columns.name: ShowLevel
columns.description: If equals 1, then display the item level next to the item name. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: levelreq
columns.description: Controls the player level requirement for being able to use the item

columns.name: reqstr
columns.description: Defines the amount of the Strength attribute needed to use the item

columns.name: reqdex
columns.description: Defines the amount of the Dexterity attribute needed to use the item

columns.name: rarity
columns.description: Determines the chance that the item will randomly spawn (1 / N) . The higher the value then the rarer the item will be. This field depends on the spawnable field being enabled, the quest field being disabled, and the item level being less than or equal to the area level. This value is also affected by the relative Act number that the item is dropping in, where the higher the Act number, then the more common the item will drop.

columns.name: spawnable
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item can be randomly spawned. If equals 0, then this item will never randomly spawn.

columns.name: speed
columns.description: For Armors: Controls the Walk/Run Speed reduction when wearing the item. For Weapons: Controls the Attack Speed reduction when wearing the item.

columns.name: nodurability
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item will not have durability. If equals 0, then the item will have durability.

columns.name: cost
columns.description: Defines the base gold cost of the item when being sold by an NPC. This can be affected by item modifiers and the rarity of the item.

columns.name: gamble cost
columns.description: Defines the gambling gold cost of the item on the Gambling UI. Only functions for rings and amulets.

columns.name: code
columns.description: Defines a unique 3* letter/number identifier code for the item. This code is case sensitive, so a69 and A69 are considered different. *Can technically use 4 characters instead of 3, but Items.json will not process it (no visuals).

columns.name: alternategfx
columns.description: Uses a unique 3 letter/number code similar to the defined code fields to determine what in-game graphics to display on the player character when the item is equipped

columns.name: namestr
columns.description: String Key that is used for the base item name. Reference string files found in local/lng/strings, such as item-names.json

columns.name: auto prefix
columns.description: Automatically picks an item affix name from a designated group value from AutoMagic.txt . Applies to all item qualities, excluding sets and uniques.

columns.name: component
columns.description: Determines the layer of player animation when the item is equipped. This uses a Token entry from Composit.txt

columns.name: hasinv
columns.description: Determines if an item can be socketed with runes, jewels or Gems. (1 = Socketable | 0 = Not Socketable)

columns.name: gemsockets
columns.description: Controls the maximum number of sockets allowed on this item. This is limited by the item's size based on the invwidth and invheight fields. This also compares with the MaxSockets1 field(s) in the ItemTypes.txt file.

columns.name: gemapplytype
columns.description: Determines which affect from a gem or rune will be applied when it is socketed into this item ( Gems.txt )

columns.name: flippyfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item in the game world when it is dropped on the ground (uses the file name as the input). *Legacy View Only

columns.name: invfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item graphics in the inventory (uses the file name as the input). *Legacy View Only

columns.name: uniqueinvfile
columns.description: Controls which DC6 file to use for displaying the item graphics in the inventory when it is a Unique quality item (uses the file name as the input). *Legacy View Only (Any text required in field to use entry from uniques.JSON for D2R view)

columns.name: Transmogrify
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item will use the transmogrify function. If equals 0, then ignore this. This field depends on the "useable" field being enabled. Does not function at all

columns.name: TMogType
columns.description: Links to a "code" field to determine which item is chosen to transmogrify this item to. Does not function at all

columns.name: TMogMin
columns.description: Controls the minimum quantity that the transmogrify item will have. This depends on what item was chosen in the "TMogType" field, and that the transmogrify item has quantity. Does not function at all

columns.name: TMogMax
columns.description: Controls the minimum quantity that the transmogrify item will have. This depends on what item was chosen in the "TMogType" field, and that the transmogrify item has quantity. Does not function at all

columns.name: useable
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item can be used with the right-click mouse button command (this only works with specific belt items or quest items). If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: type
columns.description: Points to an Code defined in the ItemTypes.txt file, which controls how the item functions

columns.name: type2
columns.description: Points to a secondary Item Type defined in the ItemTypes.txt file, which controls how the item functions. This is optional but can add more functionalities and possibilities with the item

columns.name: dropsound
columns.description: Points to a sound defined in the Sounds.txt file. Used when the item is dropped on the ground

columns.name: dropsfxframe
columns.description: Defines which frame in the flippyfile animation to play the dropsound when the item is dropped on the ground

columns.name: usesound
columns.description: Points to sound defined in the Sounds.txt file. Used when the item is moved in the inventory or used

columns.name: unique
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item can only spawn as a Unique quality type. If equals 0, then the item can spawn as other quality types

columns.name: transparent
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item will be drawn transparent on the player model (similar to ethereal models). If equals 0, then the item will appear solid on the player model

columns.name: transtbl
columns.description: Controls what type of transparency to use (if the above transparent field is enabled). Referenced by the Code value of the Transparency Table

columns.name: lightradius
columns.description: Controls the value of the light radius that this item can apply on the monster. This only affects monsters with this item equipped, not other types of units. This is ignored if the item's component on the monster is "lit", "med", or "hvy"

columns.name: belt
columns.description: Controls which belt type to use for belt items only. This field determines what index entry in the Belts.txt file to use

columns.name: autobelt
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item will automatically be placed is a free slot in the belt when picked up, if possible. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: stackable
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item will use a quantity field and handle stacking functionality. This can depend on if the item type is throwable, is a type of ammunition, or is some other kind of miscellaneous item. If equals 0, then the item cannot be stacked

columns.name: minstack
columns.description: Controls the minimum stack count or quantity that is allowed on the item. This field depends on the stackable field being enabled

columns.name: maxstack
columns.description: Controls the maximum stack count or quantity that is allowed on the item. This field depends on the stackable field being enabled

columns.name: spawnstack
columns.description: Controls the stack count or quantity that the item can spawn with. This field depends on the stackable field being enabled

columns.name: quest
columns.description: Controls what quest class is tied to the item which can enable certain item functionalities for a specific quest. Any value greater than 0 will also mean the item is flagged as a quest item, which can affect how it is displayed in tooltips, how it is traded with other players, its item rarity, and how it cannot be sold to an NPC. If equals 0, then the item will not be flagged as a quest item. Referenced by the Code value of the Quests Table

columns.name: questdiffcheck
columns.description: If equals 1 and the quest field is enabled, then the game will check the current difficulty setting and will tie that difficulty setting to the quest item. This means that the player can have more than 1 of the same quest item as long each they are obtained per difficulty mode (Normal / Nightmare / Hell). If equals 0 and the "quest" field is enabled, then the player can only have 1 count of the quest item in the inventory, regardless of difficulty

columns.name: missiletype
columns.description: Points to the *ID field from the Missiles.txt file, which determines what type of missile is used when using the throwing weapons

columns.name: spellicon
columns.description: Determines the icon asset for displaying the item's spell. This uses an ID value based on the global skillicon file. If this value equals -1, then the item's spell will not display an icon. Used as a parameter for a PSpell function

columns.name: pSpell
columns.description: Uses the Code value to select a spell from the Player Spell Table when the item is used. This depends on the item type.

columns.name: state
columns.description: Links to a State field defined in the States.txt file. It signifies what state will be applied to the player when the item is used. Used as a parameter for a PSpell function

columns.name: len
columns.description: Calculates the frame length of a state. Used as a parameter for a PSpell function

columns.name: spelldesc
columns.description: Uses the code value from the below table to format and display string for the item's tooltip:

columns.name: spelldescstr2
columns.description: Defines the secondary string key used by the above spelldesc table

columns.name: spelldesccalc
columns.description: Value applied or used by the above spelldesc table

columns.name: spelldesccolor
columns.description: Uses the Code value from the below table, for the above spelldesc table.

columns.name: durwarning
columns.description: Controls the threshold value for durability to display the low durability warning UI. This is only used if the item has durability

columns.name: qntwarning
columns.description: Controls the threshold value for quantity to display the low quantity warning UI. This is only used if the item has stacks

columns.name: gemoffset
columns.description: Determines the starting index of possible gem/rune options based on the gemapplytype field and the Gems.txt entries. For example, if this value equals 9, then the game will start with index 9 ("Chipped Emerald") and ignore lower-indexed entries, meaning the item cannot roll these options

columns.name: BetterGem
columns.description: Links to another item's Code field. Also used by Function 18 from the Code field in Shrines.txt to specify which gem is the upgraded version

columns.name: bitfield1
columns.description: Controls different flags that can affect the item. Uses an integer value to check against different bit fields by using the "&" operator. For example, if the value equals 5 (binary = 101) then that returns true for both the 4 (binary = 100) and 1 (binary = 1) bit field values.

columns.name: Transform
columns.description: Controls the color palette change of the item for the character model graphics. Referenced by the Code value of the Inventory Transform Table

columns.name: InvTrans
columns.description: Controls the color palette change of the item for the inventory graphics. Referenced by the Code value of the Inventory Transform Table

columns.name: SkipName
columns.description: If equals 1 and the item is Unique rarity, then skip adding the item's base name in its title. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: NightmareUpgrade
columns.description: Links to another item's Code field. Used to determine which item will replace this item when being generated in the NPC's store while the game is playing in Nightmare difficulty. If this field's code equals "xxx", then this item will not change in this difficulty

columns.name: HellUpgrade
columns.description: Links to another item's Code field. Used to determine which item will replace this item when being generated in the NPC's store while the game is playing in Hell difficulty. If this field's code equals "xxx", then this item will not change in this difficulty

columns.name: mindam
columns.description: The minimum physical damage provided by the item

columns.name: maxdam
columns.description: The maximum physical damage provided by the item

columns.name: PermStoreItem
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item will always appear on the NPC's store. If equals 0, then the item will randomly appear on the NPC's store when appropriate

columns.name: multibuy
columns.description: If equals 1, then use the multi-buy transaction function when holding the shift key and buying this item from an NPC store. This multi-buy function will automatically purchase enough of the item to fill up to a full quantity stack or fill the available belt slots if the item is has the autobelt field enabled. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Nameable
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item's name can be personalized by Anya for the Act 5 Betrayal of Harrogath quest reward. If equals 0, then the item cannot be used for the personalized name reward

columns.name: diablocloneweight
columns.description: The amount of weight added to the diablo clone progress when this item is sold. When offline, selling this item will instead immediately spawn diablo clone.

columns.name: normcode
columns.description: Links to the Code field to determine the normal version of the item

columns.name: ubercode
columns.description: Links to a Code field to determine the Exceptional version of the item

columns.name: ultracode
columns.description: Links to a Code field to determine the Elite version of the item
